title: Euler's Column Buckling Formula
content: The critical axial load at which a slender column buckles elastically is P_cr = π²·E·I / (K·L)², where K is the effective-length factor.
extended_content: E is Young's modulus, I is the second moment of area about the weak axis, L is the unsupported length, and K depends on end conditions (K=1 for pin-pin, K=0.5 for fixed-fixed, K=2 for fixed-free cantilever).  Valid only in the elastic (Euler) range; use Johnson's formula for stocky columns.
source: Gere & Goodno, Mechanics of Materials, 8th ed.
